mechanism_narrative: A three-month rising trend in nonfarm payrolls signals accelerating household income and consumption, which increases expected corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS 3-month change over any 2-year rolling window (Spearman rank IC <= 0).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index inflation accelerating above 4 percent year-over-year forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive monetary policy, raising real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment and declining housing starts indicate a contracting household balance-sheet channel and collapsing residential investment, which jointly depress aggregate demand and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over two months while HOUST decreases over the same period
failure_condition: When the joint condition triggers, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over individual series (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held above 4 percent during an explicit monetary tightening cycle, restrictive credit conditions dominate earnings growth expectations and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% and the Fed is in a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts near-term corporate revenue visibility and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns exhibit a negative correlation with INDPRO month-over-month change over any 1-year rolling window (Pearson r < 0).

mechanism_narrative: A three-month contraction in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand and negative operating leverage for consumer-facing corporations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of instances when RSAFS 3-month change is negative.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and stagnant or falling real GDP growth creates a restrictive financial conditions wedge that raises corporate borrowing costs precisely when revenue growth slows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS level and GDPC1 quarterly change does not significantly predict negative S&P 500 20-day returns (t-statistic magnitude < 2.0) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.3 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over two months indicates an inflection in the labor market from full employment to slack, which reduces household income expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over two months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over two months.
